text: In Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), the smallest deployable unit of computing that represents a single running process is a {{c1::Pod}}.
explanation: Think of a Pod like a sleeping bag. It can hold one person (a container) or a couple of close friends (helper containers) who share the same air (network ports) and space. You place the sleeping bags inside a bedroom (Node).
usage:
• Pods share network namespaces, IP addresses, and storage volumes.
  • Analogy: Pods = Sleeping Bags.
spanish: En Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE), la unidad de computación más pequeña que se puede implementar y que representa un único proceso en ejecución es un Pod.

text: In GKE, the virtual machine (Compute Engine instance) that actually hosts and runs the application containers is called a {{c1::Node}}.
explanation: Think of a Node like a bedroom in a big house. Each bedroom has space, beds (CPU), and drawers (RAM). You put your sleeping bags (Pods) inside this bedroom to rest. If a bedroom gets too full, you open another bedroom (Node scaling).
usage:
• Nodes run the container runtime, kubelet, and kube-proxy.
  • Analogy: Node = Bedroom.
spanish: En GKE, la máquina virtual (instancia de Compute Engine) que realmente aloja y ejecuta los contenedores de la aplicación se llama Nodo (Node).

text: To expose a group of Pods to the network under a single stable IP address, you configure a Kubernetes {{c1::Service}}.
explanation: Think of a Service like a mailman standing in the hallway. Since sleeping bags (Pods) can move to different bedrooms or change names, the mailman knows exactly where they moved. He takes the letters and delivers them to the right sleeping bag, no matter where it is.
spanish: Para exponer un grupo de Pods a la red bajo una única dirección IP estable, configuras un Servicio (Service) de Kubernetes.

text: The Kubernetes component that manages the cluster state, schedules Pods, and handles API requests is the {{c1::Control Plane}}.
explanation: Think of the Control Plane like the house captain. He has a big map (etcd database) showing where every bedroom (Node) and sleeping bag (Pod) is. If a sleeping bag gets torn, the captain tells the helpers to replace it immediately.
spanish: El componente de Kubernetes que gestiona el estado del clúster, programa los Pods y maneja las solicitudes de API es el Plano de Control (Control Plane).

text: When using Google-managed services (like Cloud SQL or Vertex AI), Google hosts these services in a hidden, Google-owned network called a {{c1::Tenant Project}}.
explanation: Think of a Tenant Project like a neighbor's backyard. We don't own it, but Google builds a tool shed there for us (like our database). To use it, we build a private gate in the fence (VPC Peering) so we don't have to walk on the public road (internet).
usage:
• Uses Private Service Access (PSA) to allocate an IP range (e.g. /16) in our VPC and peer it to Google's service producer VPC.
  • Analogy: Tenant Project = Neighbor's backyard.
spanish: Al usar servicios administrados por Google (como Cloud SQL), Google aloja estos servicios en una red oculta propiedad de Google llamada Proyecto Inquilino (Tenant Project).

text: To connect your VPC to Google-managed tenant projects using an allocated IP address range, you configure {{c1::Private Service Access (PSA)}}.
explanation: Think of PSA like buying a walkie-talkie channel. We reserve a channel number (IP range like `10.150.0.0/16`) in our room, and Google configures the neighbor's backyard system to talk on that same channel. This links our yards privately without sharing our other secrets.
usage:
• PSA is built on top of VPC Network Peering but requires an IP range allocation first.
  • Analogy: PSA = Private walkie-talkie channel setup.
spanish: Para conectar su VPC a los proyectos inquilinos administrados por Google utilizando un rango de direcciones IP asignado, configura el Acceso a Servicios Privados (Private Service Access - PSA).

text: In Google Cloud, Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) networks are global, whereas the individual IP address ranges allocated within them, called {{c1::subnets}}, are regional.
explanation: Think of a VPC like a large sandbox spanning the whole world. Subnets are chalk lines we draw on the sand in specific regions (like Iowa or Belgium) to separate our toys. Toys in the Iowa box cannot talk to toys in the Belgium box unless we let them.
usage:
• VPC = global; Subnets = regional.
  • Analogy: Subnet = Chalk box on play room floor.
spanish: En Google Cloud, las redes de Nube Privada Virtual (VPC) son globales, mientras que los rangos de direcciones IP individuales asignados dentro de ellas, llamados subredes, son regionales.

text: To control which network packets can enter or leave individual Compute Engine instances, you configure VPC {{c1::firewall rules}}.
explanation: Think of firewall rules like a security guard at the door of our playroom. He has a list (rules) stating: 'Only toys wearing green name tags (network tags) are allowed in.' If a red-tagged toy tries to enter, the guard stops them.
usage:
• GCP firewalls use network tags or service accounts to target specific VM groups dynamically.
  • Analogy: Firewall = Room security guard checking tags.
spanish: Para controlar qué paquetes de red pueden ingresar o salir de las instancias individuales de Compute Engine, configura las reglas de firewall de la VPC.

text: To allow VM instances without external IP addresses to reach Google APIs and services, you must enable {{c1::Private Google Access}} on the subnet.
explanation: Think of Private Google Access like a tunnel under the play room floor that leads directly to Google's library. Normally, VMs need to go out the front window (public IP) to get books, but this tunnel lets them walk directly inside the library privately.
usage:
• Configured per-subnet. VMs must have only internal IPs and route to private.googleapis.com or restricted.googleapis.com.
  • Analogy: Private Google Access = Secret tunnel to the library.
spanish: Para permitir que las instancias de VM sin direcciones IP externas alcancen las API y los servicios de Google, debe habilitar el Acceso Privado a Google (Private Google Access) en la subred.

text: To establish a private, unidirectional connection from your VPC to Vertex AI online prediction endpoints without VPC peering, you deploy {{c1::Private Service Connect (PSC)}}.
explanation: Think of PSC like a private pneumatic mail tube. Instead of opening our entire fence (VPC Peering) to the neighbor, we install a small tube endpoint on our desk. When we put a letter (AI query) in the tube, it sucks it directly to the wizard's tower (Vertex AI).
usage:
• PSC creates a local IP address in the consumer VPC that maps to the producer service endpoint.
  • Analogy: PSC = Pneumatic mail tube.
spanish: Para establecer una conexión privada y unidireccional desde su VPC a los endpoints de predicción en línea de Vertex AI sin el peering de VPC, implementa Private Service Connect (PSC).

text: To connect your physical on-premises network directly to GCP with a physical fiber link at a Google co-location facility, you use {{c1::Dedicated Interconnect}}.
explanation: Think of Dedicated Interconnect like building a private bullet train track directly from our school house to the Google castle. It is very fast, has huge space for traffic, and doesn't share the track with any public cars (internet).
usage:
• Provides 10 Gbps or 100 Gbps transport. Requires a physical cross-connect at a Google edge location.
  • Analogy: Dedicated Interconnect = Private train track.
spanish: Para conectar su red física local directamente a GCP con un enlace de fibra física en una instalación de co-ubicación de Google, utiliza Dedicated Interconnect.

text: To securely connect your GCP VPC to another cloud provider (like AWS) over the public internet using encrypted tunnels, you configure {{c1::Cloud VPN}}.
explanation: Think of Cloud VPN like using a secret walkie-talkie code to talk to a friend in another treehouse across town. Anyone can listen to the air (public internet), but since we speak in our secret language (IPSec encryption), only our friend can understand the words.
usage:
• Uses IPSec protocols (ESP, IKE) and propagates routes using BGP (Border Gateway Protocol).
  • Analogy: Cloud VPN = Encrypted walkie-talkie code.
spanish: Para conectar de forma segura su VPC de GCP a otro proveedor de nube (como AWS) a través de la red pública de internet mediante túneles cifrados, configura Cloud VPN.
